_comment: Gateway ACL — declares which MCP servers the host-side gateway can spawn and which trust tiers may use them. This file is gitignored; the shipped repo includes acl.example.json as a starting template.
Use the /add-mcp-to-gateway skill (or edit this file directly + restart nanoclaw) to register an MCP.
String fields support `${env:VAR}` interpolation against the host process.env at load time — useful for install-location-dependent paths.

Secrets referenced by envFromSecrets values are looked up at spawn time from groups/_gateway/mcp-secrets.json (also gitignored). The MCP subprocess sees the env var name on the left, with the secret value pulled from the JSON key on the right.

example-filesystem.description: Filesystem MCP — read/write files in an allowed directory. Replace with the MCPs you actually want.
